content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN positive integers.

A subsequence of this array is called cool if the averages of all non-empty subsequences of this subsequence are present in the complement subsequence.  The complement subsequence refers to the subsequence formed by the elements we didn't consider.

For example, consider the array A=[8,4,2,1,3,2,5,8,2]A=[8, 4, 2, 1, 3, 2, 5, 8, 2]A=[8,4,2,1,3,2,5,8,2].


Consider the subsequence [2,2,8][2, 2, 8][2,2,8] of this array.

The complement subsequence is [8,4,1,3,5,2][8, 4, 1, 3, 5, 2][8,4,1,3,5,2].

The non-empty subsequences of [2,2,8][2, 2, 8][2,2,8] are [2],[8],[2,2],[2,8],[2,2,8][2], [8], [2, 2], [2, 8], [2, 2, 8][2],[8],[2,2],[2,8],[2,2,8], with averages 2,8,2,5,42, 8, 2, 5, 42,8,2,5,4 respectively.
All of these values are present in the complement subsequence, therefore [2,2,8][2, 2, 8][2,2,8] is a cool subsequence.


Note that **no rounding is done when computing the average.** For example the average of {1,2,5}\{1, 2, 5\}{1,2,5} is 1+2+53=83\frac{1+2+5}{3} = \frac{8}{3}31+2+5‚Äã=38‚Äã, we do not round it to 222 or 333.

Find **any** cool subsequence of the array with length at least 111, or report that no such subsequence exists.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the length of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚Äî the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case,



If a cool subsequence exists in AAA, print two lines.

The first line should contain an integer KKK (1‚â§K<N1 \leq K \lt N1‚â§K<N), denoting the length of the cool subsequence you found.
The second line should contain KKK space-separated integers denoting the elements of the cool subsequence.


If no cool subsequence exists, print the single integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1 on a new line instead.

If there are multiple cool subsequences in AAA, any of them will be accepted as a valid output.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: As explained in the statement, [2,2,8][2, 2, 8][2,2,8] is a cool subsequence for this array.
Test case 222: No cool subsequence exists.
